006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.mustache;
018
019import java.io.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.*;
025
026import com.github.mustachejava.*;
027
028/**
029 * {@link ResponseProcessor} that detects {@link MustacheView}-typed return values and asks the
030 * configured {@link MustacheFactory} to render them directly onto the response writer.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Auto-registered by {@link MustacheMixin} via
034 * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Rest#responseProcessors() @Rest(responseProcessors=...)}
035 * &mdash; callers who add the mixin don't need to wire up this class explicitly. Callers who want
036 * to handle {@code MustacheView} returns <i>without</i> adopting the mixin can add this class to
037 * their own
038 * {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Rest#responseProcessors() responseProcessors} list.
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>Behavior:</h5>
041 *
042 * <ol class='spaced-list'>
043 *    <li>Inspect the response content. If the value is not a {@link MustacheView}, return
044 *       {@link ResponseProcessor#NEXT NEXT} so the rest of the chain runs.
045 *    <li>Read the active {@link MustacheMixin} from the {@code RestContext} bean store to
046 *       discover the {@link MustacheFactory} (lazy default if no factory bean is registered) and
047 *       the optional template-suffix knob.
048 *    <li>Apply every entry from {@link MustacheView#getResponseHeaders()} via
049 *       {@link jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse#setHeader(String, String)
050 *       res.setHeader(...)}.
051 *    <li>Default {@code Content-Type} to {@code text/html;charset=UTF-8} if the caller did not set
052 *       one explicitly (Mustache is content-neutral, but HTML is overwhelmingly the most common
053 *       output target; an explicit caller header wins).
054 *    <li>Call {@code factory.compile(templateName).execute(res.getWriter(), view.getAttributes())}
055 *       to stream the rendered output.
056 *    <li>When no Mustache engine is on the classpath, the {@link MustacheFactory}-typed import
057 *       here fails to load at first use and surfaces {@link #NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC} naming the
058 *       missing dependency.
059 * </ol>
060 *
061 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
062 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MustacheMixin}
063 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MustacheView}
064 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ResponseProcessors">Response Processors</a>
065 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MustacheViewSupport">Mustache View Support</a>
066 * </ul>
067 *
068 * @since 10.0.0
069 */
070@SuppressWarnings({
071   "resource" // Closeables here are framework-managed and not owned/closed by this class; not a real leak.
072})
073public class MustacheViewRenderer implements ViewRenderer {
074
075   /** Default {@code Content-Type} applied when the view does not specify one explicitly. */
076   public static final String DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE = "text/html;charset=UTF-8";
077
078   /**
079    * Diagnostic message emitted when no Mustache engine can be found on the classpath at
080    * render-time.
081    *
082    * <p>
083    * Public so tests and consumer apps can pattern-match against the message text without
084    * depending on internal string literals.
085    */
086   public static final String NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC =
087      """
088      No Mustache engine is available on the classpath. Add:
089        - com.github.spullara.mustache.java:compiler   (mustache.java — the engine the bridge targets)
090      Or register a custom @Bean MustacheFactory that picks up your preferred resolvers.
091      Note: Spring Boot's spring-boot-starter-mustache ships com.samskivert:jmustache, NOT
092      mustache.java; the bridge is mustache.java-specific. To use jmustache, supply your own
093      ResponseProcessor instead of MustacheViewRenderer.
094      See https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MustacheViewSupport for the full matrix.""";
095
096   @Override /* Overridden from ResponseProcessor */
097   public int process(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException, BasicHttpException {
098      var req = opSession.getRequest();
099      var res = opSession.getResponse();
100
101      var content = res.getContent(Object.class);
102      if (! (content instanceof MustacheView view))
103         return NEXT;
104
105      // Resolve the bridge resource (carries MustacheFactory + cached default + templateSuffix).
106      // Fall back to a fresh MustacheMixin when the renderer is used standalone without
107      // the mixin.
108      var bridge = req.getContext().getBeanStore()
109         .getBean(MustacheMixin.class)
110         .orElseGet(MustacheMixin::new);
111
112      // Apply caller-supplied response headers first so a caller-provided Content-Type wins
113      // over the bridge's default below.
114      view.getResponseHeaders().forEach(res::setHeader);
115      if (! res.containsHeader("Content-Type"))
116         res.setHeader("Content-Type", DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE);
117
118      var templateName = bridge.applyTemplateSuffix(view.getTemplateName());
119      try {
120         var factory = bridge.resolveMustacheFactory(req);
121         var mustache = factory.compile(templateName);
122         mustache.execute(res.getWriter(), view.getAttributes()).flush();
123         return FINISHED;
124      } catch (LinkageError ex) {
125         throw new InternalServerError(ex, NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC);
126      } catch (IOException ex) {
127         throw ex;
128      } catch (Exception ex) {
129         throw new InternalServerError(ex, "Mustache render failed for ''{0}''", templateName);
130      }
131   }
132}
